title: podman container restore error
body:
### Issue Description

I hope podman will create checkpoint and restore from checkpoint. Unfortunatly, podman can't restore from the checkpoint for unexpected reason.(maybe from criu) Here are more details. 
restore.log(end of the log)
(00.081134) pie: 1: 	mmap(0x7fff72bbe000 -> 0x7fff72bc0000, 0x7 0x32 -1)
(00.081137) pie: 1: Preadv 0xebb80000:462848... (105 iovs)
(00.086280) Error (criu/cr-restore.c:1492): 18962 stopped by signal 11: Segmentation fault
(00.086947) mnt: Switching to new ns to clean ghosts
(00.091040) Error (criu/cr-restore.c:2447): Restoring FAILED.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.podman build -t myjava .
2.podman run --security-opt="seccomp=unconfined" --tmpfs /tmp -d --name test myjava
3.podman container checkpoint test
4.podman container restore -k test



### Describe the results you received

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47500029/234455099-3d631908-a758-436f-a68e-e0a24b1fa874.png)
[restore_log.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/11329691/restore_log.txt)


### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
I set up a count program that  increments the output every 5 seconds, here is output:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47500029/234456985-8d71d52d-7234-4e03-a6b2-15078bef3741.png)

And I hope Podman can continue to increment the number after restoring.
Dockerfile:
```
FROM ubuntu:18.04
RUN apt-get update 
RUN apt-get install -y openjdk-8-jdk 
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* 
ENV JAVA_HOME /usr/lib/jvm/java-8-openjdk-amd64
ENV PATH $JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH
COPY Main.java /home/test/
WORKDIR /home/test/
RUN javac Main.java
CMD ["java","Main"]
```
Main.java
```
import java.util.Timer;
import java.util.TimerTask;
import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;

public class Main {
    private static int counter = 0;
    public static void main(String[] args){
        Timer timer = new Timer();
        timer.schedule(new TimerTask() {
            @Override
            public void run() {
                counter ++ ;
                System.out.println(counter);
            }
        },0,5000);
    }
}
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Works corretly with podman v4.5 and criu v3.17.1 on my fedora 37 laptop,
I suggest you update to a more recent version.
